title: lepidium lasiocarpum subsp. wrightii
intro: Lepidium lasiocarpum subsp. wrightii, also known as Wright's peppergrass or desert peppergrass, is a subspecies of the annual plant Lepidium lasiocarpum. This plant is a member of the mustard family and is native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The leaves and stems of Lepidium lasiocarpum subsp. wrightii have been used in traditional medicine, and they are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, and aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional cooking, where its peppery flavor is used to add zest to dishes.
